persona.expertise: TypeScript, REST APIs, Linear/Jira SDKs, Database Migrations, Email/Notification Services

task.objective: Implement MCP tools that integrate the dashboard with external project management and notification systems
task.success_criteria: Two functional MCP tools that transform the dashboard from a passive reporter into an active participant in the team's triage workflow, enabling automatic issue creation and failure assignment.

context.background: Teams currently must manually create tickets when tests fail and manually assign ownership. These tools automate the workflow, reducing triage time and ensuring accountability for broken builds.

existing_infrastructure.database: Neon PostgreSQL with test_executions, test_results tables
existing_infrastructure.notifications: Email capability via Resend or similar service (to be configured)

integrations.external: Linear, Jira (optional based on org configuration)

functional.requirement: Create Linear or Jira tickets populated with test failure details
Assign failure investigation to team members within the dashboard
Store external issue IDs back in the dashboard database
Send email or Slack notifications on assignment
Support auto-assignment rules based on test file paths or tags

non_functional.requirement: Security: Store API keys securely in organization settings
Reliability: Handle external API failures gracefully
Audit: Log all issue creation and assignment actions

tool.name: create_issue_from_failure
tool.purpose: Creates a Linear or Jira ticket populated with failure details

input_schema.field: The execution ID containing the failure
Specific test name (if omitted, creates issue for overall execution failure)
Optional additional context to include in the issue body
Email of team member to assign (must be org member)

implementation_logic.step: Fetch context using get_execution_details: - Test failure details and stack trace - Branch name and commit info - Flakiness history for this test
Format issue description as markdown: - Stack trace in code block - Link to dashboard execution view - Flakiness statistics if available - Artifacts list (logs, screenshots, traces)
Call external API (Linear or Jira): - Use API key from organization settings - Create issue with formatted content - Set labels/tags as configured
Link back to dashboard: - Store external_issue_id in test_results or new linking table - Enable bidirectional navigation

tool.name: assign_failure
tool.purpose: Assigns a failure investigation to a team member within the dashboard

input_schema.field: Email of the team member to assign
Whether to send notification to assignee

implementation_logic.step: Validate user_email is a member of the organization
Update database: - Set assigned_to field on execution or test_result record - Set assigned_at timestamp - Store assignment notes if provided
Send notification (if notify=true): - Email via Resend/Postmark - Include: execution link, failure summary, any notes - Optional: Slack webhook if configured

output.error: Error message (e.g., "User not found in organization")

migration.name: add_workflow_fields
migration.description: Schema changes needed to support workflow tools

example.description: Creating a Linear ticket from a test failure
example.user_query: File a ticket for this flaky test

agent_steps.step: Agent identifies the failed test from context
Tool fetches failure details, formats markdown
Tool creates Linear issue via API
Returns: "Created ticket LIN-123: Flaky Test - Checkout Flow"

generated_issue.title: Flaky Test: checkout.spec.ts - Payment validation timeout
generated_issue.body:
## Test Failure Details
**Test**: `checkout.spec.ts` > "should complete payment flow" **Execution**: [#456](https://dashboard.exolar.dev/executions/456) **Branch**: `feature/new-checkout` **Commit**: `abc1234`
## Error
``` TimeoutError: Waiting for selector "[data-testid=payment-success]" exceeded timeout of 30000ms ```
## Flakiness History
- Failed 3 times in last 7 days - Pass rate: 85% (17/20 runs)
## Artifacts
- [Screenshot](https://r2.exolar.dev/...) - [Trace](https://r2.exolar.dev/...)

example.description: Assigning a failure to a team member

input.notes: Likely related to the API changes from yesterday

email_sent.subject: Assigned: Test Failure Investigation #789
email_sent.body:
You have been assigned to investigate a test failure.
Execution: #789 - login-flow.spec.ts Status: Failed (3 tests) Link: https://dashboard.exolar.dev/executions/789
Notes from assigner: "Likely related to the API changes from yesterday"

security.rule: Store external API keys encrypted in database, never in code
Validate user_email is a member of the authenticated organization
Log all issue creation and assignment actions for audit

integration.rule: Handle rate limits from Linear/Jira APIs gracefully
Support both Linear and Jira, configured per-organization
Fail gracefully if external service is unavailable

avoid.item: Hardcoding API keys or webhook URLs
Creating duplicate issues for same failure
Assigning to users outside the organization
Sending notifications without user consent (respect notify flag)

output_format.code: TypeScript implementation files to add to packages/mcp-server/src/: - tools/create-issue-from-failure.ts - tools/assign-failure.ts - integrations/linear-client.ts - integrations/jira-client.ts - services/notification-service.ts
output_format.migrations: SQL migration script for database schema changes: - scripts/0XX_add_workflow_fields.sql
output_format.tests: Integration tests for external API calls (with mocks)
output_format.documentation: Update packages/mcp-server/README.md with workflow tool documentation

roi_analysis.time_saved: Medium - Faster than manual copy-paste ticket creation
roi_analysis.collaboration: High - Enforces ownership of broken builds
roi_analysis.complexity: High - Requires external API auth management and DB schema changes
